_comment: REAL HACKATHON DEMO DATA — generated from local notebook exports. AOI-level signal only; not field-level proof.
challengeName: Treefera LCAW 2026 Hackathon — Grinnell Iowa Regen Ag Detection
tagline: Detecting regenerative agriculture signals from baseline-harmonised satellite time-series data
satellite: Sentinel-2 L2A monthly median optical reflectance, baseline-harmonised

confidence.note: Growing-season NDVI rises after known practice-change periods, but this is AOI-level evidence. Field-level target/control polygons are still required.

factors.label: Baseline-harmonised NDVI
factors.detail: Sentinel-2 optical bands were corrected by subtracting 1000 on/after 2022-01-25 before NDVI calculation.

factors.label: Growing-season NDVI uplift
factors.detail: AOI mean growing-season NDVI rises from 0.6078 pre-adoption to 0.6814 in the no-till period, an uplift of +0.0736 (12.1%).

factors.label: Cover crop transition signal
factors.detail: Mean growing-season NDVI during the 2021–2022 cover-crop period is 0.621, slightly above the 2017–2020 baseline of 0.6078.

factors.label: Biomass context
factors.detail: Biomass layers are included as AOI-level context only; they are coarse/noisy and not treated as proof of practice change.

factors.label: Target/control polygons pending
factors.detail: Current GeoJSON is an 8x8 km AOI box, not individual Roger/control field boundaries.

aiInsight.headline: AOI-level vegetation signal strengthens after practice changes
aiInsight.body: Across the Grinnell AOI, baseline-harmonised Sentinel-2 growing-season NDVI increases from 0.6078 before adoption to 0.6814 during the no-till period. This is a useful screening signal, but it is not proof of regenerative agriculture without field-level target/control comparison.
aiInsight.disclaimer: Signal, not proof. This is AOI-level evidence and should be validated with field boundaries, controls, weather, crop rotation, and management records.

limitations: Current GeoJSON is an AOI boundary, not individual field polygons.
AOI-level NDVI can mix multiple farms, crop rotations, and management regimes.
Weather, rainfall timing, crop type, and harvest dates can mimic vegetation changes.
Biomass layers are coarse/noisy context and are not field-level proof.
A credible next step is target-vs-control field comparison using Roger's field and matched neighbouring fields.
nextSteps: Acquire or manually define Roger's target field and 3–5 matched conventional neighbour polygons.
Recalculate NDVI per field rather than across the full AOI.
Compare post-2021 and post-2023 vegetation behaviour against controls.
Add rainfall/weather and crop rotation controls.
Validate signals against farm management records or ground observations.

before.label: Before — pre-practice-change
before.caption: Placeholder visual. Replace with a notebook-exported Sentinel-2 composite if time allows.

after.label: After — post-practice-change
after.caption: Placeholder visual. Replace with a notebook-exported Sentinel-2 composite if time allows.
